explanation: This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in the PULLUP state and the system is supported and sensor data is good, then in the next time step the autopilot will be in the TRANSITION state.  It uses the index 1 template, which represents the next state operator (X).  bool_exp2 represents the condition of being in PULLUP state, supported system and good sensor data. bool_exp3 represents the transition to the TRANSITION state.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in the PULLUP state (bool_exp2), it will eventually transition to the TRANSITION state within N_DURATION time units (bool_exp3).  The condition for this transition is that the system is supported and sensor data is good. This is captured using the implication (->) operator in the G (always) modality.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in the PULLUP state (bool_exp2) and the system is supported (supported) and sensor data is good (good), then in the next state, the autopilot will be in the TRANSITION state (bool_exp3).  The N_DURATION is set to 1 to reflect the immediate state change requirement. If the supported and good conditions are not met, the implication is vacuously true, which is acceptable as we are only interested in the behavior of the system when those conditions hold.

This LTL formula states that whenever the autopilot is in the PULLUP state (bool_exp2) and the system is supported (supported) and sensor data is good (good), then in the next state, the autopilot will transition to the TRANSITION state (bool_exp3).  The N_DURATION value is 1, indicating that the transition should occur immediately in the next step.

This LTL property states that whenever the system is supported (bool_exp2) and sensor data is good (bool_exp1),  then eventually within N_DURATION time units, the autopilot will transition to the TRANSITION state (bool_exp3).

This LTL property states that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good, the autopilot will eventually transition to the TRANSITION state within N_DURATION time units. It captures the requirement by specifying the conditions (supported and good) under which a state change is expected and using F[0, N_DURATION] to define the time bound for the transition.

This LTL property checks that whenever the autopilot is in the PULLUP state (bool_exp2) and the system is supported and sensor data is good (bool_exp1), then in the next time step the autopilot will transition to the TRANSITION state (bool_exp3).  It uses template 11 to express this next state transition and ensures the condition is globally true.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in the PULLUP state and the system is supported and the sensor data is good, then in the next state, the autopilot will be in the TRANSITION state.  It uses the chosen template to capture the causality of the next state transition.

This LTL specification asserts that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good (bool_exp2), the autopilot will eventually transition to the TRANSITION state within N_DURATION time units (bool_exp3).  The use of G implies that this property must hold globally across all time steps. The choice of template 14 accounts for the fact that the transition to TRANSITION is not immediate but occurs within a bounded time window after the condition is met. This considers possible delays or processing within the autopilot system.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in the PULLUP state (bool_exp2) and the system is supported (supported) and sensor data is good (good), then in the next state, the autopilot will be in the TRANSITION state (bool_exp3). It uses template 11, which is well-suited for expressing a state transition under specific conditions.

This LTL property states that when the system is supported and sensor data is good, then the autopilot will eventually transition from the PULLUP state to the TRANSITION state within N_DURATION time units.  It uses template 14 to express the implication between the condition and the eventual state change, ensuring that the transition happens only when the system is healthy and the sensor data is reliable. If the system is not supported or the sensor data is bad, the property doesn't make any claim about the state change.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in the PULLUP state (bool_exp2), and the system is supported (supported) and sensor data is good (good), then it will eventually transition to the TRANSITION state (bool_exp3) within N_DURATION time units. It uses template 14.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in the PULLUP state (bool_exp2) and the system is supported (supported) and sensor data is good (good), then in the next state the autopilot will be in the TRANSITION state (bool_exp3).  The formula uses the G operator to globally enforce the implication over all time steps.

This LTL specification asserts that whenever the system is supported (supported) and sensor data is good (good), the autopilot will eventually transition to the TRANSITION state (state_is_TRANSITION) within N_DURATION time units.  It uses template 19. The antecedent (bool_exp2) checks for supported and good conditions. The consequent (bool_exp3) checks for the state_is_TRANSITION. The G operator ensures that this implication holds globally at all times.

This LTL specification asserts that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good, the autopilot will eventually transition to the TRANSITION state within N_DURATION time units.  It uses template 14 which asserts that a transition occurs under given condition within a specific timeframe.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in the PULLUP state (bool_exp2), and the system is supported (supported) and sensor data is good (good), then in the next state, the autopilot transitions to the TRANSITION state (bool_exp3).  The N_DURATION parameter is set to 1, meaning the implication holds immediately in the next state.

This LTL property states that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good, the autopilot will eventually transition to the TRANSITION state within N_DURATION time units.  It uses the implication operator (->) to specify that whenever bool_exp2 is true, bool_exp3 must eventually become true. bool_exp2 captures the condition of supported system and good sensor data, and bool_exp3 captures the autopilot's transition to the TRANSITION state. The G operator ensures that this implication holds globally (at every time instant). The F operator with a bounded duration checks if the event will occur within that specific period.

This LTL property states that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good (bool_exp2), the autopilot will eventually transition to the TRANSITION state within N_DURATION time units (bool_exp3). It uses template 14.

This LTL formula states that whenever the autopilot is in the PULLUP state (bool_exp2) and the system is supported (supported) and sensor data is good (good), then in the next state the autopilot will be in the TRANSITION state (bool_exp3).  The formula uses the G operator to ensure that this implication holds globally, meaning that at every time instant the implication must be true. The choice of template 11 accounts for the fact that the transition from PULLUP to TRANSITION is not instantaneous but occurs over one step.

This LTL specification asserts that whenever the system is supported (bool_exp2) and sensor data is good (bool_exp1), the autopilot will eventually change states from PULLUP to TRANSITION within N_DURATION time units.  The atomic propositions are combined to check for the correct conditions, and the "X" operator checks the state in the next step. If the condition of supported and good sensor data is never met, it won't affect the validity of the property because of the implication in the template.

This LTL property states that, if the system is supported and sensor data is good, then the autopilot will eventually change states from PULLUP to TRANSITION within N_DURATION time units.  The boolean expression bool_exp2 represents the condition "supported & good", and bool_exp3 represents the condition that the autopilot changes from state PULLUP to TRANSITION. The template ensures that the transition happens only when the conditions are met, and it allows a grace period of N_DURATION time units before declaring a failure to transition.

This LTL specification asserts that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good, the autopilot will eventually transition from the PULLUP state to the TRANSITION state within N_DURATION time units.  It uses template 14 to express this. bool_exp2 represents the condition that the autopilot is in the PULLUP state and the system is supported and sensor data is good. bool_exp3 represents the autopilot being in the TRANSITION state.  The G operator ensures that this implication holds globally (at all times), and the F operator ensures that the TRANSITION state will eventually be reached within a certain time bound.

This LTL specification asserts that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good (bool_exp2), the autopilot will eventually transition to the TRANSITION state (bool_exp3) within N_DURATION time units. This captures the requirement that the autopilot changes states from PULLUP to TRANSITION under the specified conditions.  The use of F[0, N_DURATION] ensures that the transition occurs within a reasonable timeframe, preventing indefinite delays.

This LTL property states that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good, the autopilot will eventually transition to the TRANSITION state within N_DURATION time units.  It uses template 14 to express this. bool_exp2 represents the condition of the system being supported and the sensor data being good. bool_exp3 represents the autopilot being in the TRANSITION state. It ensures that if the condition in bool_exp2 is true, then within a limited time (N_DURATION), bool_exp3 will become true.

This LTL specification asserts that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good, the autopilot will eventually transition from the PULLUP state to the TRANSITION state within N_DURATION time units.  bool_exp2 represents the condition where the system is supported and sensor data is good (supported & good). bool_exp3 represents the condition that the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state (state_is_TRANSITION). The template ensures that if bool_exp2 is true, then eventually (F) bool_exp3 will become true within the specified duration.  If the condition never holds, the formula does not restrict system behavior.

The chosen template ensures that whenever bool_exp2 is true, in the next step bool_exp3 will be true.

The chosen template 14 accounts for the possibility of this transition not always happening at the next state but rather within some duration.  If the conditions are never met, the property holds trivially.

This LTL specification asserts that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good, the next state will be TRANSITION, given that the current state is PULLUP.  It uses the index 11 template. bool_exp2 represents the condition of being in PULLUP state and the system being supported and sensor data being good. bool_exp3 represents the next state being TRANSITION. The formula ensures this transition happens in the next time step.

This LTL property states that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good, then the next state will be TRANSITION.  It uses the chosen template to express the conditional next-state behavior. bool_exp2 represents the precondition that the system is supported and sensor data is good, and bool_exp3 represents the postcondition that the autopilot's next state is TRANSITION. The choice of template 1 ensures that the transition happens in the next step.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in the PULLUP state (bool_exp2), it will eventually transition to the TRANSITION state (bool_exp3) within N_DURATION time units, given that the system is supported and sensor data is good (bool_exp1).  The G operator ensures that this implication holds globally across all time points.  The F operator means that eventually (within the specified time window) the TRANSITION state will be reached.

The N_DURATION parameter is set to 1 to enforce the immediate state change after satisfying conditions.

The boolean expression bool_exp2 checks for supported and good conditions. bool_exp3 checks if the current state is TRANSITION. This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in the PULLUP state (bool_exp2), it will eventually transition to the TRANSITION state (bool_exp3) within N_DURATION time units, given that the system is supported and sensor data is good (bool_exp1). The formula uses the G (always) operator to ensure that the implication holds for all states. It uses the F (eventually) operator to state that eventually the transition to the TRANSITION state must occur, and the interval operator [0,N_DURATION] to bound this to occur within a bounded time frame, thus ensuring the transition occurs relatively promptly.

This LTL specification asserts that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good, the next state will be TRANSITION.  It uses the chosen template to express this. bool_exp2 represents the condition of supported system and good sensor data, and bool_exp3 indicates the next state being TRANSITION.

This LTL property states that whenever the system is supported and sensor data is good, the autopilot will eventually transition from the PULLUP state to the TRANSITION state within 1 time unit.  The chosen template uses the G (p -> F q) structure to express that globally (G), if the condition (p) is true, then eventually (F) the desired state (q) will occur. The condition p consists of the system being supported and sensor data being good (supported & good). The desired state q indicates that the autopilot is in TRANSITION. N_DURATION is set to 1 to indicate that the change is expected within one time unit.

This captures the natural language requirement by specifying that the transition should occur under the given conditions and providing a time bound for the transition.

The N_DURATION is set to 1 to reflect the immediate transition.

It uses the atomic propositions provided and combines them to express the required state transition under the given conditions.

The formula uses the G (always) operator to specify that this should hold for all time steps, and the F (eventually) operator to allow for a delay in the transition.

It uses template 14 because it directly expresses a response (TRANSITION) to a condition (supported and good) within a time bound. bool_exp2 represents the condition of the system being supported and sensor data being good. bool_exp3 represents the condition of the autopilot changing states from PULLUP to TRANSITION.

We use the next operator X to capture the "when...then in the next state." The G operator ensures this property holds globally, meaning it should always be true at every point in time.

This captures the requirement that the autopilot changes states from PULLUP to TRANSITION under the specified conditions.  The use of X (next) operator directly addresses the "shall change states" requirement. The use of implication (->) makes this template highly suitable for expressing this requirement. Boolean expressions ensure the property is easily readable and understandable by the LTL interpreter.
